SEARCH

Class MenuManager

java.lang.Object
MenuManager
public class MenuManager extends Object
Manages all menu displays and user input collection. Provides a centralized interface for presenting various menu/submenu options to the user and capturing their selections. \
Version:
3.0
Author:
Christopher Perez
See Also:
  • Scanner

  • Field Details

    • scnr

      private Scanner scnr
      Scanner instance for reading user input from standard input

  • Constructor Details

    • MenuManager

      public MenuManager()
      Constructs a new MenuManager instance.

      Initializes the Scanner for reading user input from standard input (System.in). The Scanner is created with System.in as the input source and will remain open for the lifetime of this MenuManager instance.

  • Method Details

    • showMainMenu

      public int showMainMenu()
      Displays the main menu and returns the user's choice.

      Shows the primary menu options available in the application:

      • 1 - Exit the program
      • 2 - Open a data file (Pokemon CSV file)
      • 3 - Search for a specific character by name
      • 4 - Find characters by attributes (hit points, speed)
      • 10 - Run unit testing

      Returns:
      the integer value representing the user's selected menu option. Valid options are 1, 2, 3, 4, or 10. Returns 0 for invalid input.
      See Also:
      • getUserInput()

    • showSubMenu

      public int showSubMenu()
      Displays the sub menu for file operations and returns the user's choice.

      Shows options for unit testing file operations:

      • 1 - Print the first 7 and last 7 lines of the loaded file
      • 2 - Write all unique character names to a file

      This menu is displayed when the user selects the Unit Testing option from the main menu.

      Returns:
      the integer value representing the user's selected option (1 or 2). Returns 0 for invalid input.
      See Also:
      • showMainMenu()
      • getUserInput()

    • showSecondarySubMenu

      public int showSecondarySubMenu()
      Displays the secondary sub menu for character attribute searches.
      Returns:
      the integer value representing the user's selected attribute type. Valid values are 1 (hit points) or 2 (speed). Returns 0 for invalid input.
      See Also:
      • showCharacterAttributesSpecificHitMenu()
      • showCharacterSpeedValueMenu()
      • getUserInput()

    • showCharacterAttributesSpecificHitMenu

      public int showCharacterAttributesSpecificHitMenu()
      Displays the menu for searching characters by hit point values.
      Returns:
      the integer value representing the user's selected search option. Valid values are 1-5. Returns 0 for invalid input.
      See Also:
      • showSecondarySubMenu()
      • getUserInput()

    • showCharacterSpeedValueMenu

      public int showCharacterSpeedValueMenu()
      Displays the menu for searching characters by speed values.
      Returns:
      the integer value representing the user's selected speed search option. Valid values are 1-8. Returns 0 for invalid input.
      See Also:
      • showSecondarySubMenu()
      • getUserInput()

    • getUserInput

      private int getUserInput()
      Safely retrieves integer input from the user.
      Returns:
      the integer value entered by the user. Returns 0 if the input is invalid or cannot be parsed as an integer.
      See Also:
      • Scanner.nextInt()
      • Scanner.nextLine()
